previewTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate: Constantino Aucca Chutas - Episode 3

title.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate: Constantino Aucca Chutas - Episode 3

text.value: As part of his massive reforestation drive, 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate Constantino Aucca Chutas has mobilized thousands of people from villages throughout the Andes. His community-based programmes have been responsible for planting 4.5 million native trees in the mountains of his homeland, Peru. Through his second non-governmental organization, Acción Andina, he is spreading this momentum internationally, reforesting the Andes in the hopes of preserving the mountain ecosystems of the region.

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Constantino Aucca Chutas - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: A descendant of Quechuan farmers, biologist Constantino Aucca Chutas felt compelled to act 30 years ago when he saw the damage being done to the forests of Peru as a result of uncontrolled logging, wildfires and land trafficking.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Constantino Aucca Chutas - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Constantino Aucca Chutas - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: High Andean Polylepis forests are major contributors to water security from the Andes to the Amazon. Growing as high as 5,000 metres above sea level, they not only help mitigate soil erosion and provide a habitat for wildlife, but also act as a natural “water tower” on a giant scale.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Saving Brazil's forests and bird life - Video

pageCaption.value: Anita Studer, a Swiss ornithologist who went to Brazil to study its rich array of bird life, has, through sheer determination and dedication, inspired Brazilians to save their forests and changed thousands of lives for the better in what is now her second home.

metadescription.value: Since 1990, biologist Anita Studer has launched a variety of programmes to protect Brazil's forests and bird population. Watch the video on rolex.org.
